person.name: Konrad Rzentarzewski
person.bio: I was there when the config burned. In the age before observability, chaos reigned. The servers howled, and nothing scaled but entropy. Then kube cattle were born — simple, nameless beasts born of children’s games. But they multiplied. And their hunger grew vast. Years passed. The sun revolved the Earth. Then the Earth revolved the Cloud. I grew tired of debugging in darkness. I longed for light. And logs. Preferably structured. That’s when I began preaching — not loudly, just… declaratively. One apply at a time. I walked among DevOps tribes, where bash begat shell, and shell begat pain. To them I brought ConfigMaps, and Secrets hidden in plain sight. Then came the mysterious YAML — a language too holy to lint — inscribed deep within the API’s own heart. Through toil and merge, the infrastructure became manifest. Pods floated on the waters. Deployments begat replicas. Helm bore Charts. Git begat Ops. What was once dev spun to prod. And every service had a name — and every name resolved. Unless external DNS was involved. And the Preachers said: \"Behold, our logs streameth clean.\" Thus began the age of the declarative. We once dwelled in darkness. Now, we scale planetary in full light.

person.name: Ashok Srirama
person.bio: Ashok is a Containers Specialist Solutions Architect at AWS with 18+ years of experience in software engineering and distributed systems. He has led multiple cloud migration projects over the past 10+ years and now focuses on helping customers leverage Kubernetes for Gen AI workloads. He specializes in serverless applications, containers, and cloud architecture. Outside work, he enjoys cricket and driving.

person.name: Gábor Szabó
person.bio: Gabor is a seasoned Cloud Solution Architect specializing in Kubernetes, Security, and Azure. As a consultant, he helps companies modernize their applications through containerization across public cloud and on-premises environments. He is passionate about emerging technologies and enjoys evangelizing them at conferences and in hands-on workshops. Gabor is excited about innovations and he loves to integrate complex components to form something new, sometimes a bit strange :) but usually a streamlined solution.

person.name: Lucas Gonçalves Mendes De Gois
person.bio: With over 7 years of experience in the tech industry, I am a dedicated IT professional and an enthusiastic advocate of open-source culture. I have extensive expertise in Infrastructure as Code, utilizing advanced tools to streamline and automate complex systems. My background includes hands-on experience with Kubernetes and managing public cloud environments, enabling efficient and scalable solutions. I continuously seek opportunities to innovate and contribute to community-driven projects, always pushing the boundaries of modern technology.

person.name: Claudiu Avatamanitei
person.bio: I am an experienced IT professional with a strong background in design and implementation of complex software platforms, reliability and observability engineering, and defense in depth security. I am currently focused on enabling digital and cloud-native transformations at scale in the mortgage servicing business, by providing harmonious foundational technology ecosystems composed of vendor neutral, CNCF managed open source de facto standards. With extensive hands on expertise in Kubernetes (EKS, OCP), Istio, GitOps and ArgoCD, OTEL and Kiali, IaC (Terraform, Crossplane, Spacelift, Puppet), software development (Python, Java, C++), and several CI/CD platforms, I enable teams to focus on business value delivery and ensure low stress, successful development and operations of comprehensive software platforms.

person.name: Nadi Durna
person.bio: I'm Nadi from Turkey. I've been working as a DevOps engineer for about 4 years. Throughout these years, I've focused on deepening my Kubernetes expertise — it has always been my primary area of growth. I'm passionate about running everything on Kubernetes whenever possible, which sometimes frustrates my customers and colleagues due to the complexity it introduces — though I have to admit, that complexity feels perfectly natural to me. 😄

person.name: Yosef Arbiv
person.bio: I am married to Adi, father of three, and OpenTelemetry Group Leader at Cisco. My group and I are promoting OpenTelemetry adoption via contributions to the project, publishing blog posts on OpenTelemetry Blog (https://opentelemetry.io/blog/) & Cisco Tech Blog (https://techblog.cisco.com/tags/opentelemetry), and running OpenTelemetry talks & workshops at public events.Why I am excited to be an ambassador: I want to promote OpenTelemetry and enable more people to use and contribute to it. I aim to improve the development processes in OpenTelemetry and to make the OpenTelemetry community more welcoming and supportive of new developers. I want to continue advocating for OpenTelemetry by promoting it at global events and helping others do so.

person.name: Emna Drissi
person.bio: I'm a Solution Architect specializing in designing and implementing scalable, secure solutions that meet business needs. I work closely with stakeholders to bridge the gap between technical and business requirements, ensuring the right technologies and architectures are in place for success. I am passionate about Kubernetes and cloud native technologies, and enjoy leveraging them to build modern, efficient systems.

person.name: Fernando Ordoñez Bravo
person.bio: I am an Electronics and Telecommunications Engineer by profession, and a passionate technology enthusiast at heart. I began my career as a network administrator, implementing telecommunications networks in Data Centers. Over time, I developed a strong interest in cloud computing—particularly AWS—where I gained extensive experience and validated my knowledge through certifications in SysOps, Architecture, and Security. Today, I am part of the team that administers the Containerization Platform at a financial institution, working in a fascinating field with a promising future.

person.name: Jules Pichon
person.bio: With a keen interest in information security and automation, I am eager to discover and learn about the technologies of tomorrow. Perhaps the boundary between the worlds of cybersecurity and artificial intelligence is closer than it seems. Who knows?

person.name: Kacper Cesarz
person.bio: I'm a Cloud Engineer and DevOps enthusiast with a strong focus on CNCF technologies. My journey with CNCF began with Kubernetes, and since then I’ve gained hands-on experience with tools like ArgoCD and Istio. I’m passionate about continuous improvement and draw inspiration from lean principles and practices. I'm actively involved in the CNCF community and always looking to share knowledge and grow through meetups and collaboration.

person.name: Lei Hou
person.bio: As a Senior Cloud & DevOps Engineer, I am passionate about automation and different Cloud techniques. My expertise spans infrastructure as code, Kubernetes, and other plugins and softwares in the CNCF ecosystem. Besides that, the advanced techniques from different Cloud solutions also fascinate me. With these tools, I can build a scalable, reliable, and secure infrastructure and Apps. I’m also interested in collecting different certifications to deepen my knowledge and broaden my horizons.

person.name: Stathis Kapaniaris
person.bio: My name is Stathis Kapaniaris and I am a Software and Cloud Engineer, originally coming from Greece and based in Vienna, Austria. My everyday working life is around Kubernetes eco-system and various other CNCF-projects. I visit frequently CNCF hosted events, such as Kubecon and KCD. Aside from professional usage, I also use Kubernetes in private, home made projects and publish tutorials about them in https://devs-in-the-cloud.medium.com/

person.name: Thomas Vitale
person.bio: Thomas Vitale is a software engineer with a focus on building cloud native solutions, currently working at Systematic in Denmark. He is the author of the "Cloud Native Spring in Action" book and plays an active role in the cloud native ecosystem as a CNCF Ambassador, Co-Chair of the CNCF Application Development Working Group, Oracle ACE Pro, and Testcontainers Community Champion.A strong advocate of open source collaboration, Thomas contributes to various projects in the Java and cloud-native space, including Spring AI, Testcontainers, Carvel, Dapr, and Buildpacks. He is also passionate about sharing his expertise with the community through talks, workshops, and writing on technical topics.
